data.title: Masimo named a new top idea at Piper on potential turnaround
data.description:
Piper Sandler named Masimo Corporation (NASDAQ:MASI) as a new top idea on Friday, noting that the pulse oximetry company will soon reach a significant reset in its margins and earnings, outperforming other stocks in its coverage.

With an overweight recommendation and a $165 price target, analyst Jason Bednar said that MASI has further room to rise even after a 30% rally over the past month since the recent shareholder vote and other developments, including management updates.

“Not only do we see a further move higher as additional components of the MASI investment case are de-risked, but we also expect an earnings power inflection in 25/26 as greater for this stock than any other in our coverage universe,” Bednar wrote.

Additionally, citing upcoming catalysts outside the core business improvements, such as a possible resolution to the Apple (APPL) lawsuit and a new CEO appointment, the analyst pointed to potentially favorable changes to the company’s risk-reward profile.

data.title: FanDuel acquires naming rights to Bally's regional sports channels
data.description:
• Online gaming platform FanDuel (NYSE:FLUT) and Diamond Sports Group, a subsidiary of Sinclair (NASDAQ:SBGI) have announced a long-term commercial partnership that will result in a name change for Diamond Sports regional sports networks ("RSN") as FanDuel Sports Network.
  • On October 21, The Bally Sports RSNs will officially be renamed as FanDuel Sports Network with their corresponding regional monikers.
  • In addition, the partnership will also give FanDuel (FLUT) reseller designation for Diamond’s direct-to-consumer product, as well as linear and digital media placement and integration opportunities across all live NBA, NHL, and MLB games on the FanDuel sports network.
  • “Partnering with Diamond provides us an opportunity to put the FanDuel brand at the intersection of the nation’s largest group of regional sports networks. A large cohort of FanDuel customers are devoted [regional sports network] viewers and this agreement allows us to further cement the FanDuel brand with sports fans and provides a unique vehicle to reward our users,” said Mike Raffensperger, president of FanDuel Sports.

data.title: U.S. drilling rigs tick higher in Baker Hughes weekly report
data.description:
Active rigs drilling for oil and gas in the U.S. fell by 1 to 585, ticking lower for the fourth time in five weeks, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest weekly report.

Drilling rigs targeting crude oil in the U.S. added 1 to 482, while gas rigs dropped by 2 to 99 and 4 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

Baker Hughes said the total rig count is down by 39 rigs, or 6%, compared with this time last year.

data.title: CVS could face long arduous turnaround like Walgreens
data.description:
CVS Health’s (NYSE:CVS) turnaround could be long and arduous, if it resembles that of drugstore rival Walgreens Boots Alliance (NASDAQ:WBA).

Earlier Friday, CVS (CVS) issued a Q3 profit warning and announced it was replacing CEO Karen Lynch with David Joyner, president of its Caremark pharmacy benefit management, or PBM, business.

The management change comes as the healthcare conglomerate grapples with rising costs in its health insurance business, increased federal scrutiny of its PBM business, and slowed sales at its flagship drug stores.

It also comes amid a major restructuring and speculation the company may divest its Aetna insurance business, which it acquired in 2018 for $69B. Last week, CVS (CVS) also announced it was exiting its core infusion services business.

Walgreens (WBA) and CVS (CVS) stocks have both been hammered this year, with Walgreens (WBA) shares plunging 60% and CVS (CVS) shares tumbling 25% since the beginning of January. In contrast, the S&P500 has risen 23%.

But Walgreens (WBA) shares have shown signs of life in recent days, thanks to a better-than-expected fiscal Q4 earnings report issued on Oct. 15, with shares rallying 17% over the past five days.

In conjunction with its earnings release, Walgreens (WBA) has also announced aggressive plans to restructure the company, including the shuttering of 1,500 stores over the next three years.

Walgreens (WBA) has also been mulling selling all or part of its majority stake in primary care network VillageMD due to the business's ongoing cash requirements, according to an SEC filing in August. Walgreens (WBA) bought a controlling stake in VillageMD in 2021 for $5.2B, but has reportedly closed over 140 clinics in recent months.

Walgreens (WBA) has also been whittling down its stake in drug distributor Cencora (COR), using the proceeds to help pay down debt. Walgreen's is reportedly Cencora's (COR) largest customer.

SA analyst Vincent Phan said in his recent analysis that he believes Walgreens (WBA) stock has “bottomed out," adding that its fiscal Q4 earnings call “showed a clear commitment to a turnaround, especially with management putting a focus on its core retail pharmacy business and planning to close 1,200 unprofitable stores.”

data.title: Lamb Weston gains as activist Jana Partners pushes for sale (update)
data.description:
Update 2pm: Adds Jana, Continental Grain confirmation, updates shares.

Lamb Weston (NYSE:LW) jumped 7% after Jana Partners disclosed it has built a stake and wants the french-fry maker to explore a sale.

Jana disclosed a 5% stake in Lamb Weston (LW) and Continental Grain has a less than 1% stake and Jane said it intends to have discussions with the board and management. The activist has concerns about capital spending, share repurchase activist and compensation practices.

"The litany of self-inflicted missteps that have led to underperformance for shareholders," Jana wrote in a 13D filing disclosing the stake.

Jana may decide to nominate candidates for the board and is working with industry executives including former Lamb Weston (LW) executive chairman, Timothy McLevish, according to a WSJ report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Jana believes that Lamb Weston (LW), which has attracted potential buyers over the years, could be a target for larger food companies, according to the WSJ report. Jana has teamed up with agricultural investment firm Continental Grain on its LW investment.

"We are aware of the 13D filings from JANA Partners and Continental Grain," Lamb Weston said in a statement emailed to Seeking Alpha. "We regularly engage with our shareholders to better understand and consider their views and will continue to do so."

The Jana push comes after Lamb Weston (LW) shares sunk almost 30% on July 24 after the french-fry maker missed earnings expectations with its FQ4 report and warned of a tough path ahead. Shares of the $10 billion company have dropped 34% this year.

Earlier this month, Lamb Weston (LW) announced it's cutting 4% jobs globally, shuttering an older plant in Connell, Washington, and will temporarily curtail certain production lines in North America in a restructuring move to reduce operating expenses.

The WSJ news on Lamb Weston (LW) also comes as activist investors, including Jana, are set to present their ideas at the 13D Monitor Active/-Passive Investor Summit in New York City on Tuesday.

data.title: SLB slides as oil explorers expected to slow spending growth
data.description:
SLB (NYSE:SLB) -3.8% in Friday's trading after edging past consensus Q3 adjusted earnings estimates but warning of weak spending by international oil explorers as customers exercise caution in an environment of lower commodity prices.

Q3 net income edged higher to $1.19B, or $0.83/share, from $1.12B, or $0.78/share, in the year-earlier quarter, while revenues rose 10% to $9.16B.

By segment, Q3 revenues in well construction fell 3.4% to $3.31B, production systems surged 31% to $3.1B, reservoir performance increased 8.5% to $1.82B, and digital and integration added 10.8% to $1.09B.

Weak oil and gas prices have caused "a cautionary approach to activity and discretionary spend by many customers," CEO Olivier Le Peuch said on SLB's (SLB) earnings conference call.

However, the company reiterated its expectation to deliver full-year adjusted EBITDA margin at or above 25%, partly helped by cost cutting.

In 2025, SLB (SLB) foresees international market spending increasing at a low to mid-single digit percentage rate, while North American spending should come in flat to slightly down.

SLB (SLB), which amasses ~80% of its revenues from overseas markets, said Q3 international revenue rose 12% Y/Y to $7.42B, helped by increased sales in Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Iraq and Kuwait, as well as in North Africa, compared to an 18% Y/Y increase during the previous three quarters.

Q3 North America revenues rose 3% to $1.68B due to higher activity in the U.S. Gulf of Mexico.

The company also said its just-announced sale of its interests in the Palliser Block in Alberta, Canada, will generate ~$430M in cash proceeds and reduce well-abandonment liabilities.

data.title: Bitcoin may have already frontloaded its gains during its last recovery – analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is not making consistent new highs in the likes of gold (GLD), (XAUUSD:CUR), and its lack of momentum is notable, said Jurrien Timmer, director of global macro at Fidelity.

“Perhaps it already frontloaded those gains when it recovered from its dip to $15,000 in late 2022,” he wrote in an X post.

He said that both gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and bitcoin (BTC-USD) are “different players on the same team” but maybe they are not playing together.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD), he said, aspires to have the utility of money – “I think of it as exponential gold (XAUUSD:CUR), (GLD), or gold with an exponential network feature – but for bitcoin to compete with gold its adoption curve needs to continue to grow.

He said that according to Metcalfe’s Law, if the adoption curve continues to grow, its valuation and price should grow as well. However, bitcoin’s price has “followed a boom-bust cycle in which it tends to dramatically overshoot and undershoot the underlying curve,” which makes sense only for an asset that is still young and ongoing price discovery.

Credits to Fidelity

Moreover, this year, bitcoin’s (BTC-USD) curve has flattened. It has stayed at around 60,000 to 70,000 since late February, and with the number of addresses with a value of at least $1 stagnating at the 46M mark, said Timmer.

Only due to its steep gain, at the beginning of the year (due to the onset of ETFs) the index is up 55.65% year-to-date.

If bitcoin (BTC-USD) has indeed become a mature asset that is less prone to boom-bust swings, it will be up to the shape of its adoption curve to drive the price further,” Timmer concluded. “Perhaps that acceleration is coming soon if the world is heading to a state of fiscal dominance, but for now it’s gold (XAUUSD:CUR), (GLD) that’s stealing the show.”

data.title: Gilead to pull Trodelvy U.S. indication on trial setback
data.description:
• Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) on Friday announced plans to voluntarily withdraw a U.S. indication for its antibody-drug conjugate Trodelvy in bladder cancer after a late-stage confirmatory trial for the drug failed in May.
  • The indication approved under the FDA’s accelerated pathway in 2021 allowed the use of Trodelvy as a late-line option for adult patients with locally advanced or metastatic urothelial cancer.
  • Data on tumor response rate and duration of response, as found in GILD’s Phase 2 TROPHY-U-01 study, backed the initial approval.
  • Gilead (NASDAQ:GILD) said its decision, taken in discussions with the FDA, will not affect other indications approved in the U.S. for Trodelvy.
  • The setback was expected after Gilead’s (GILD) Phase 3 TROPiCS-04 trial for Trodelvy failed to reach the primary endpoint of overall survival (OS) in the intention-to-treat (ITT) population in May.

data.title: Immigration impact on GDP negative under Trump, positive under Harris: Brookings
data.description:
The impact of immigration policy on US gross domestic product will be more negative under a second Donald Trump administration than one led by Kamala Harris, according to the Brookings Institute.

The left-leaning think tank in a report this week analyzed the possible scenarios for net migration under either administration and their likely macroeconomic implications.

Brookings created a "high immigration" and a "low immigration" scenario for each presidential candidate. "These scenarios reflect a combination of the historical record under the Trump administration and the Biden-Harris administration, announced and inferred immigration policies, as well as our judgment of likely developments."

The think tank expects net immigration to be "significantly higher" under a Harris government than a second Trump term. Brookings' high end of its immigration estimate under Harris next year is 3.7M, while the low-end estimate for a Trump administration is -740K.

Brookings said the direct impact on GDP by its high-end estimate of net immigration under Harris would be a one-percentage point gain while it would slide four-percentage point under a Trump low.

“Given the long-term trends of fertility rates and population aging in the U.S., immigration is projected to be an increasingly important factor underlying labor force growth,” the report said. “Policies that constrain immigration would shrink the size of the labor force.”

Brookings said productivity has been shown to rise due to immigration, but its estimates did not incorporate that data.

“The consequences for the aggregate economy are clear, but the fiscal effects of immigration are also worth noting,” the report said. “Immigration improves the federal budget because immigrants pay more in federal income and payroll taxes than they use in federal benefits.”

data.title: Cadence Design, Synopsys being 'left out' from chip winners: Baird
data.description:
• Cadence Design Systems (NASDAQ:CDNS) and Synopsys (NASDAQ:SNPS) are being "left out" as investors pick the "winners" in the semiconductor space that has been fueled by artificial intelligence, investment firm Baird said.
  • "Historically, when chip stocks underperform on cycle concerns, [electronic design automation] attracts interest and sees outperformance against SOX of ~20% (median) to ~30% (average)," analyst Joe Vruwink wrote in a note to clients. "This time around, [electronic design automation] stocks are lagging, with many debates and ASML commentary contributing pressure. Ultimately, R&D expenditure and [electronic design automation] investment likely prove more resilient than stocks seem to be discounting, with verification a bigger contributor in FY25 than FY24; both items likely outweigh China concerns (which we have now better accounted for in modeling)."
  • Vruwink has Outperform ratings on Cadence Design and Synopsys, but lowered his price targets to $332 and $644 from $338 and $663, respectively.
  • Both companies have already taken the risk of any contribution from China coming up short with their outlook for the rest of 2024, but it's also "pragmatic" to think that China is below growth in 2025 as well, Vruwink added. As such, he now expects both companies to grow revenues between 13% and 14% year-over-year, with "mid-teens" growth excluding China.
  • Cadence Design shares have lost 1.5% year-to-date, while Synopsys has gained 1.5%.
